title: Aggregated Summary
text: use . user constructs table scratch choosing dropdowns, typing free text, entering dates. Rows can added, deleted, reset. Use dataset exist yet user author. flexible_table_ui() returns named list (table, add_row_button, reset_button) element can placed anywhere layout independently. Column spec quick reference *Omitting type defaults "select" backward compatibility. server returns reactive data frame complete, non-blank rows. Internal helper columns (id, delete) stripped automatically. Pass initial_data = reactive(df) pre-populate startup.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)  variety_map <- list(   apple  = c("Fuji" = "fuji", "Gala" = "gala"),   banana = c("Cavendish" = "cavendish"),   pear   = c("Bosc" = "bosc", "Bartlett" = "bartlett") )  col_specs <- list(   list(     name    = "fruit",     label   = "Fruit",     type    = "select",     choices = c("Select..." = "", "Apple" = "apple",                 "Banana" = "banana", "Pear" = "pear")   ),   list(     name                 = "variety",     label                = "Variety",     type                 = "select",     choices              = c("Select..." = ""),     depends_on           = "fruit",     get_filtered_choices = function(val) {       c("Select..." = "", variety_map[[val]] %||% character(0))     }   ),   list(     name        = "notes",     label       = "Notes",     type        = "text",     placeholder = "Optional...",     width       = 220   ) )  ui <- page_sidebar(   sidebar = sidebar(     flexible_table_ui("picker")$add_row_button,     br(),     flexible_table_ui("picker")$reset_button   ),   flexible_table_ui("picker")$table,   card(     card_header("Selected (complete rows only)"),     verbatimTextOutput("flexible_result")   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   selected <- flexible_table_server(     id                 = "picker",     col_specs          = col_specs,     duplicates_allowed = FALSE   )   output$flexible_result <- renderPrint(selected()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Aggregated Summary
text: use . existing data frame want user flag subset rows single click. per-cell inputs — output character vector selected row IDs. annotator_super_simple_ui() returns named list (table, reset_button). Key behaviour notes Selections persist across reactive updates source_data. row disappears (e.g. due filter) reappears, stays selected. return value contains IDs currently visible source_data. Pass additional reactable() arguments via reactable_options = list(...). include data, selection, onClick, rowStyle, columns, defaultSelected, elementId, theme — owned module.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)  queue <- reactive({   data.frame(     id      = 1:6,     patient = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol", "Dave", "Eve", "Frank"),     status  = c("pending", "urgent", "pending", "done", "urgent", "pending"),     age     = c(34, 52, 29, 61, 45, 38),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) })  ui <- page_sidebar(   sidebar = sidebar(     annotator_super_simple_ui("review")$reset_button   ),   annotator_super_simple_ui("review")$table,   card(     card_header("Selected IDs"),     verbatimTextOutput("simple_result")   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   selected_ids <- annotator_super_simple_server(     id                = "review",     source_data       = queue,     row_id            = "id",     display_cols      = c("patient", "status", "age"),     selection         = "multiple",     reactable_options = list(searchable = TRUE, striped = TRUE)   )   output$simple_result <- renderPrint(selected_ids()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Aggregated Summary
text: use . existing data frame want user fill structured metadata row: dropdowns, checkboxes, text fields, numbers, radio buttons. annotator_table_ui() returns single reactableOutput() element. example also demonstrates gating: notes field greyed non-interactive approved checkbox row checked. Column type reference types accept label, width, col_def_options, gates (name checkbox column controls input’s interactivity). server returns reactive data frame row_id column plus input annotation columns, filtered rows least one input touched. Display columns excluded. Pass initial_values = reactive(df) pre-seed annotations.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)  source_data <- reactive({   data.frame(     car = c("Mazda RX4", "Hornet 4 Drive", "Valiant", "Merc 240D", "Fiat 128"),     mpg = c(21.0, 21.4, 18.1, 24.4, 32.4),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) })  col_specs <- list(   list(name = "car", type = "display", label = "Car"),   list(name = "mpg", type = "display", label = "MPG"),   list(     name    = "category",     type    = "select",     label   = "Category",     choices = c("Economy" = "economy", "Sport" = "sport", "Luxury" = "luxury")   ),   list(name = "approved", type = "checkbox", label = "Approved?"),   list(     name        = "notes",     type        = "text",     label       = "Notes",     placeholder = "Reason for approval...",     gates       = "approved"   ) )  ui <- page_fluid(   h4("Car Annotation"),   annotator_table_ui("cars"),   br(),   card(     card_header("Touched rows only"),     verbatimTextOutput("annotator_result")   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   result <- annotator_table_server(     id          = "cars",     source_data = source_data,     row_id      = "car",     col_specs   = col_specs   )   output$annotator_result <- renderPrint(result()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Aggregated Summary
text: use . data grouped (age bands, project phases, departments) want user first pick items grouped table left, annotate selected items right. module composes annotator_table internally. milestone_tracker_ui() returns complete two-column bslib layout — individual element placement needed. Key behaviour notes Clicking group header row Card 1 selects rows group. Card 2 appears least one row selected. Annotation state preserved row deselected re-selected. group_sort_col controls group sort order (hidden display). NULL, groups sort alphabetically group_col.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)  milestones <- reactive({   data.frame(     id         = 1:8,     age        = c("6 months",  "6 months",  "12 months", "12 months",                    "18 months", "18 months", "24 months", "24 months"),     age_months = c(6, 6, 12, 12, 18, 18, 24, 24),     milestone  = c(       "Sits with support", "Babbles",       "Pulls to stand",    "Says mama",       "Walks alone",       "Points to objects",       "Runs steadily",     "Uses 2-word phrases"     ),     domain     = c("Motor", "Language", "Motor", "Language",                    "Motor", "Language", "Motor", "Language"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) })  annotation_specs <- list(   list(name = "milestone", type = "display", label = "Milestone"),   list(name = "domain",    type = "display", label = "Domain"),   list(     name    = "status",     type    = "select",     label   = "Status",     choices = c(       "Achieved" = "achieved",       "Emerging" = "emerging",       "Not yet"  = "not_yet"     )   ),   list(name = "notes", type = "text", label = "Notes") )  ui <- page_fluid(   milestone_tracker_ui("tracker"),   br(),   card(     card_header("Touched annotations"),     verbatimTextOutput("tracker_result")   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   result <- milestone_tracker_server(     id                   = "tracker",     data                 = milestones,     row_id               = "id",     group_col            = "age",     group_sort_col       = "age_months",     item_col             = "milestone",     badge_col            = "domain",     badge_colors         = list(       Motor    = list(bg = "#EAF3DE", text = "#27500A"),       Language = list(bg = "#DEE9F3", text = "#0A2750")     ),     annotation_col_specs = annotation_specs,     card1_title          = "Select Milestones",     card2_title          = "Record Progress",     count_label          = "milestones"   )   output$tracker_result <- renderPrint(result()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Aggregated Summary
text: server functions accept reactable_theme (reactableTheme object) reactable_options (named list extra reactable() arguments) customise rendering without modifying module internals.

what: Simple Row Select with Annotator Table
title: Annotator Super Simple
text: module thin wrapper around reactable() function reactable package. annotator_super_simple_ui annotator_super_simple_server functions just things. Easier Row Selection - field individual row active selection deselection just mouse click provides ‘reset ’ button via ui portion module. makes server side filtering easier. filter rows table make selections selected rows persist. basic pattern straightforward — pass reactive data frame, name row-ID column, list columns display:
code: library(shiny) library(reactable) library(reactableShiny)  mtcars_data      <- mtcars mtcars_data$car  <- rownames(mtcars) rownames(mtcars_data) <- NULL  display_cols <- c("car", "mpg", "cyl", "hp", "wt")  ui_els <- annotator_super_simple_ui("demo")  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("mtcars Explorer"),   fluidRow(     column(2, ui_els$reset_button),     column(10)   ),   br(),   ui_els$table,   br(),   strong("Selected cars:"),   verbatimTextOutput("selected_out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   selected <- annotator_super_simple_server(     id           = "demo",     source_data  = reactive(mtcars_data),     row_id       = "car",     display_cols = display_cols   )    output$selected_out <- renderPrint({     ids <- selected()     if (length(ids) == 0) cat("None") else cat(ids, sep = "\n")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Annotator Super Simple
text: source_data reactive expression, can drive filter control. Rows selected temporarily hidden filter remain selected reappear — module reconciles visible row indices stored ID vector every render.
code: library(shiny) library(reactable) library(reactableShiny)  mtcars_data      <- mtcars mtcars_data$car  <- rownames(mtcars) rownames(mtcars_data) <- NULL  display_cols <- c("car", "mpg", "cyl", "hp", "wt")  ui_els <- annotator_super_simple_ui("demo")  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("mtcars Explorer"),   fluidRow(     column(3,       selectInput(         "cyl_filter",         "Filter by cylinders",         choices  = c("All" = "all", "4" = "4", "6" = "6", "8" = "8"),         selected = "all"       )     ),     column(3, br(), ui_els$reset_button),     column(6)   ),   ui_els$table,   br(),   strong("Selected cars:"),   verbatimTextOutput("selected_out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    # source_data is now reactive — it changes when the filter changes.   # This is what exercises the module's persistence logic.   filtered_data <- reactive({     if (input$cyl_filter == "all") {       mtcars_data     } else {       mtcars_data[mtcars_data$cyl == as.integer(input$cyl_filter), ]     }   })    selected <- annotator_super_simple_server(     id           = "demo",     source_data  = filtered_data,     row_id       = "car",     display_cols = display_cols   )    output$selected_out <- renderPrint({     ids <- selected()     if (length(ids) == 0) cat("None") else cat(ids, sep = "\n")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Annotator Table
text: provide source data frame list column specifications (col_specs). spec declares whether column read-display column interactive input — select, checkbox, text, number, radio. module renders everything single reactable table returns reactive data frame annotations rows touched user. things worth knowing: Annotations survive filtering. source_data reactive expression can change (e.g. driven search box), annotations rows temporarily disappear preserved restored rows return. touched rows returned. module’s return value excludes rows every input still default, don’t filter blanks . Gating built . input column can declare gates = "my_checkbox" interactivity controlled checkbox row. checkbox unchecked gated inputs grey instantly — Shiny round-trip needed.

title: Annotator Table
text: Attach UI placeholder annotator_table_ui() start server logic annotator_table_server(). server function returns reactive can observe pass downstream. Running app produces table car, mpg, cyl columns shown plain text, Category dropdown, Approved? checkbox, Notes field row. output$result updates real time show rows least one input filled .
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)  col_specs <- list(   list(name = "car",      type = "display",  label = "Car"),   list(name = "mpg",      type = "display",  label = "MPG"),   list(name = "cyl",      type = "display",  label = "Cylinders"),   list(name = "category", type = "select",   label = "Category",        choices = c("Economy" = "economy", "Sport" = "sport", "Truck" = "truck")),   list(name = "approved", type = "checkbox", label = "Approved?"),   list(name = "notes",    type = "text",     label = "Notes",        placeholder = "Optional comment...") )  ui <- page_fluid(   h4("Review cars"),   annotator_table_ui("cars"),   hr(),   h4("Annotations so far"),   tableOutput("result") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   cars_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(mtcars, var = "car")   source  <- reactive(cars_df)    annotations <- annotator_table_server(     id          = "cars",     source_data = source,     row_id      = "car",     col_specs   = col_specs   )    output$result <- renderTable(annotations()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Annotator Table
text: Every column type also accepts optional label (shown column header; defaults name) width pixels.

title: Annotator Table
text: Use gates make one checkbox control whether inputs row interactive. useful “flag row” checkbox unlock additional detail fields. , Reason Notes greyed Flag review? checked. Unchecking restores previous values — never cleared.
code: col_specs <- list(   list(name = "car",    type = "display",  label = "Car"),   list(name = "flag",   type = "checkbox", label = "Flag for review?"),   list(name = "reason", type = "select",   label = "Reason",        choices = c("Price" = "price", "Safety" = "safety"),        gates   = "flag"),   list(name = "notes",  type = "text",     label = "Notes",        placeholder = "Details...",        gates   = "flag") )

title: Annotator Table
text: code full self-contained app used live demo. Copy new R session run shinyApp(ui, server) try interactively. uses representative set developmental milestone rows: check Met? mark milestone achieved, add short note — annotation panel updates real time show rows touched.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)  milestones <- data.frame(   id        = 1:10,   age       = c("6 months",  "6 months",  "12 months", "12 months",                 "18 months", "18 months", "24 months", "24 months",                 "36 months", "36 months"),   category  = c("Motor", "Language", "Motor", "Language",                 "Motor", "Language", "Motor", "Language",                 "Motor", "Language"),   milestone = c(     "Sits with support",          "Babbles consonants",     "Pulls to stand",             "Says mama/dada with meaning",     "Walks alone",                "Points to objects of interest",     "Runs without falling",       "Uses 2-word phrases",     "Jumps with both feet",       "Speaks in 3-word sentences"   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  col_specs <- list(   list(name = "id",        type = "display",  label = "ID",        width = 50),   list(name = "age",       type = "display",  label = "Age",       width = 110),   list(name = "category",  type = "display",  label = "Category",  width = 120),   list(name = "milestone", type = "display",  label = "Milestone", width = 340),   list(name = "met",       type = "checkbox", label = "Met?",      width = 60),   list(     name        = "notes",     type        = "text",     label       = "Notes",     placeholder = "Short note...",     width       = 200   ) )  ui <- page_fluid(   theme = bs_theme(version = 5),   card(     card_header("Developmental Milestones"),     card_body(annotator_table_ui("milestones"))   ),   card(     card_header("Annotations so far"),     card_body(tableOutput("annotations_out"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   source_data <- reactive(milestones)    annotations <- annotator_table_server(     id          = "milestones",     source_data = source_data,     row_id      = "id",     col_specs   = col_specs   )    output$annotations_out <- renderTable({     ann <- annotations()     if (nrow(ann) == 0) {       return(data.frame(Message = "No milestones annotated yet."))     }     dplyr::left_join(ann, milestones, by = "id") |>       dplyr::select(id, age, category, milestone, met, notes)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: flexible_table_ui() flexible_table_server() r shiny module leverage reactable package facilitate dynamic data entry. Reactable acts scaffold user input. user establishes choices select dropdown passed reactable table selection. user can add rows, delete rows, reset table. module returns reactive data frame current selections.

what: Simple Example: People Who Live in Your Home
title: Using the flexible_table module
text: Let’s say survey want know lives household user. list possible people includes : also curious know people household living.
code: home_occupants <- c(   "mother",   "father",   "grandmother",   "grandfather",   "sibling",   "sibling (male)",   "sibling (female)",   "step-mother",   "step-father",   "partner",   "spouse") professions <- c(   "doctor",   "lawyer",   "professor",   "astronaut",   "chef",   "dog walker" )

previous_headings: Overview > Simple Example: People Who Live in Your Home
title: Using the flexible_table module
text: Every dropdown table described column spec — named list minimum name, label, choices: example, collecting information home_occupant profession.
code: col_specs <- list(   list(     name    = "home_occupants",     label   = "Home Occupants",     choices = setNames(       c(1:length(home_occupants)),       home_occupants     )   ),   list(     name = "professions",     label = "Profession",     choices = setNames(       c(1:length(professions)),       professions           )   ) )

previous_headings: Overview > Simple Example: People Who Live in Your Home
title: Using the flexible_table module
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(purrr) library(reactable) library(reactableShiny)  home_occupants <- c(   "mother",   "father",   "grandmother",   "grandfather",   "sibling",   "sibling (male)",   "sibling (female)",   "step-mother",   "step-father",   "partner",   "spouse")  professions <- c(   "doctor",   "lawyer",   "professor",   "astronaut",   "chef",   "dog walker" )  col_specs <- list(   list(     name    = "home_occupants",     label   = "Home Occupants",     choices = setNames(       c(1:length(home_occupants)),       home_occupants     )   ),   list(     name = "professions",     label = "Profession",     choices = setNames(       c(1:length(professions)),       professions           )   ) )  ui <- page_fluid(   do.call(tagList, flexible_table_ui("example")),   verbatimTextOutput("result") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   selected <- flexible_table_server("example", col_specs)    output$result <- renderPrint(selected()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: flexible_table_ui() returns named list rather single UI element. lets place table buttons independently layout: want three elements together one place, .call(tagList, ...) works shortcut:
code: ui <- bslib::page_fluid(   bslib::layout_columns(     col_widths = c(2, 10),     div(       flexible_table_ui("my_table")$add_row_button,       br(),       flexible_table_ui("my_table")$reset_button     ),     flexible_table_ui("my_table")$table   ) ) ui <- bslib::page_fluid(   do.call(tagList, flexible_table_ui("my_table")) )

previous_headings: Overview > Simple Example: People Who Live in Your Home
title: Using the flexible_table module
text: flexible_table_server() returns reactive data frame current table contents. default behavior exclude rows every cell blank. feature can controlled parameter called duplicates_allowed. default value duplicates_allowed FALSE.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    selected <- flexible_table_server(     id        = "my_table",     duplicates_allowed = FALSE,     col_specs = col_specs   )    # selected() is a reactive data frame — use it like any other reactive   observe({     print(selected())   })  }

previous_headings: Overview > Simple Example: People Who Live in Your Home
title: Using the flexible_table module
text: example, can see user can add many ‘siblings’ ‘mothers’ ‘fathers’ like. , however, want disallow duplicates, can well.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)  home_occupants <- c("mother", "father", "grandmother", "grandfather", "sibling", "sibling (male)", "sibling (female)", "step-mother", "step-father", "partner", "spouse")  col_specs <- list(   list(     name    = "home_occupant_id",     label   = "Home Occupants",     choices = setNames(       c(1:length(home_occupants)),       home_occupants     )   ) )  ui <- page_fluid(   do.call(tagList, flexible_table_ui("example")),   verbatimTextOutput("result") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   selected <- flexible_table_server("example", col_specs, duplicates_allowed = FALSE)    output$result <- renderPrint(selected()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: Pass reactive data frame initial_data pre-fill table. useful editing existing record — load saved values table, user makes changes, read updated values back . Column names initial_data must match name fields col_specs. initial_data reactive — reactive expression fires (example, user switches different record), table replaced new data automatically.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    # A reactive that returns the data to load — could come from a database,   # a file, or another reactive in your app     selected <- flexible_table_server(     id           = "my_table",     col_specs    = col_specs,     initial_data = existing_data   )  } library(shiny) library(bslib) library(reactableShiny)   home_occupants <- c("mother",   "father",   "grandmother",   "grandfather",   "sibling",   "sibling (male)",   "sibling (female)",   "step-mother",   "step-father",   "partner",   "spouse")     col_specs <- list(   list(     name    = "home_occupant_id",     label   = "Home Occupants",     choices = setNames(       c(1:length(home_occupants)),       home_occupants     )   ) )  ui <- page_fluid(   do.call(tagList, flexible_table_ui("example")),   verbatimTextOutput("result") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {     existing_data <- reactive({     data.frame(       home_occupant_id = c(2,1)     )   })        selected <- flexible_table_server(     "example",      col_specs,      initial_data = existing_data,     duplicates_allowed = FALSE)    output$result <- renderPrint(selected()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: Passing data frame zero rows triggers reset single blank row. useful switching edit mode add mode:
code: existing_data <- reactive({   if (input$mode == "add") {     data.frame()          # zero rows — signals a reset   } else {     load_record(input$record_id)   } })

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: Set duplicates_allowed = FALSE silently remove duplicate rows returned data frame. Two rows considered duplicates every column value identical: Note duplicates removed returned reactive — user can still see interact table .
code: selected <- flexible_table_server(   id                 = "my_table",   col_specs          = col_specs,   duplicates_allowed = FALSE )

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: default module returns rows least one value chosen. Completely empty rows — initial blank row user touches anything — excluded. downstream code requires every column filled , set complete_rows_only = TRUE: table summarises setting affects rows returned:
code: selected <- flexible_table_server(   id                 = "my_table",   col_specs          = col_specs,   complete_rows_only = TRUE )

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: two options compose independently: returns fully complete rows, duplicates among removed.
code: selected <- flexible_table_server(   id                 = "my_table",   col_specs          = col_specs,   duplicates_allowed = FALSE,   complete_rows_only = TRUE )

title: Using the flexible_table module
text: table uses theme_bare default — minimal transparent theme inherits styling surrounding page. apply different reactable::reactableTheme(), pass reactable_theme:
code: my_theme <- reactable::reactableTheme(   backgroundColor = "#f8f9fa",   borderColor     = "#dee2e6" )  selected <- flexible_table_server(   id              = "my_table",   col_specs       = col_specs,   reactable_theme = my_theme )

title: Extends reactable tables for use with shiny (ie uploading data)
text: goal reactableShiny combine unique features reactable package shiny inputs users can easily write data databases. Package Website (development)

title: Extends reactable tables for use with shiny (ie uploading data)
text: can install development version reactableShiny GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("matthewcurrier/reactableShiny")

title: Enriched annotator — server — annotator_enriched_server
text: Server-side logic row-selection module per-row enrichment fields. Clicking anywhere row selects deselects . Enrichment inputs (dropdowns, text boxes, date pickers, number inputs) always rendered greyed-non-interactive unselected rows. Values entered enrichment fields preserved even row subsequently deselected `source_data` changes reactively.

title: Enriched annotator — server — annotator_enriched_server
code: annotator_enriched_server(   id,   source_data,   row_id,   display_cols,   enrich_specs,   initial_enrichments = NULL,   initial_selected = character(0),   reset_to = NULL,   selection = "multiple",   reactable_theme = theme_bare,   reactable_options = list() )

title: Enriched annotator — server — annotator_enriched_server
text: id `character(1)`. Shiny module namespace ID. source_data `reactive`. reactive expression returning source data frame. Must contain column named `row_id` columns named `display_cols`. row_id `character(1)`. name column `source_data` uniquely identifies row. Values coerced character stable comparison across re-renders. display_cols `character`. character vector column names `source_data` show read-columns. Column order follows order vector. enrich_specs `list`. non-empty list enrichment column specifications. element named list minimum: `name` `character(1)`. Variable name Shiny input ID stem. `type` `character(1)`. One `"select"`, `"text"`, `"date"`,     `"number"`. `label` `character(1)`. Column header label. Additional optional fields type: `choices` (select) Named character vector options. name     display label; value stored. include placeholder,     use `c("Select..." = "")` — never `c("" = "")`. `placeholder` (text) Placeholder string shown empty input. `min`, `max` (date) Strings `"YYYY-MM-DD"` format. `min`, `max` (number) Numeric bounds number input. `width` () Column width pixels, forwarded     [reactable::colDef()]. initial_enrichments `data.frame` `NULL`. pre-populated enrichments frame seed module's internal state first render. Must structure frame produced [initial_enrichments_blank()]: `row_id` column (character) followed one column per enrichment spec. Rows absent `source_data` silently ignored; rows `source_data` absent receive untouched defaults. Pass `NULL` (default) start fully blank state. initial_selected `character`. character vector `row_id` values appear selected first render. Defaults `character(0)` (nothing pre-selected). Values present `source_data` silently dropped. Used restore checkbox state re-opening existing report. reset_to `reactive` `NULL`. optional reactive expression returning named list two elements: `selected` character vector `row_id` values restore     new selection. Pass `character(0)` `NULL` clear     selections. `enrichments` data frame pre-populated enrichment values     structure `initial_enrichments`, `NULL` start     fully blank enrichment frame. reactive fires, module replaces internal `selected_ids` `enrichments` state supplied seeds forces full table re-render. correct mechanism resetting module Shiny session switches different data contexts (e.g. opening Report B Report ) without page refresh. Defaults `NULL` (mid-session reset). recommended calling pattern pass `reactiveVal(NULL)` starts `NULL` set genuine context switch occurs: selection `character(1)`. Either `"multiple"` (default) `"single"`. reactable_theme [reactable::reactableTheme()] applied table. Defaults `theme_bare`. reactable_options `list`. Additional arguments passed [reactable::reactable()] via [base::.call()]. include `data`, `selection`, `onClick`, `rowStyle`, `columns`, `defaultSelected`, `elementId`, `theme` — owned module.
code: reset_signal <- shiny::reactiveVal(NULL)   shiny::observeEvent(data_r(), {     reset_signal(list(       selected    = build_initial_selected(data_r()),       enrichments = build_initial_enrichments(data_r())     ))   }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

title: Super simple annotator — server — annotator_super_simple_server
text: Server-side logic lightweight row-selection module. Renders [reactable::reactable()] table clicking row (checkbox) toggles selection state. Selection persists across re-renders table — `source_data` changes reactively table re-renders, previously selected rows restored via `defaultSelected`.

title: Super simple annotator — server — annotator_super_simple_server
code: annotator_super_simple_server(   id,   source_data,   row_id,   display_cols,   selection = "multiple",   reactable_theme = theme_bare,   reactable_options = list() )

title: Super simple annotator — server — annotator_super_simple_server
text: id `character(1)`. Shiny module namespace ID. Must match `id` passed [annotator_super_simple_ui()]. source_data `reactive`. reactive expression returning source data frame. Must contain column named `row_id` columns named `display_cols`. row_id `character(1)`. name column `source_data` uniquely identifies row. Values coerced character stable comparison across re-renders. display_cols `character`. character vector column names `source_data` show read-columns table. Column order matches order vector. selection `character(1)`. Either `"multiple"` (default) `"single"`. Controls whether user can select one many rows. reactable_theme [reactable::reactableTheme()] object applied rendered table. Defaults `theme_bare`. reactable_options `list`. named list additional arguments passed directly [reactable::reactable()] via [base::.call()]. Useful `sortable`, `searchable`, `striped`, `groupBy`, etc. include `data`, `selection`, `onClick`, `rowStyle`, `columns`, `defaultSelected`, `theme` — owned module cause duplicate argument error included .

title: Super simple annotator — server — annotator_super_simple_server

title: Annotator table — server — annotator_table_server
text: Server-side logic Shiny module renders [reactable::reactable()] table combining read-display columns source data frame interactive input columns. module returns reactive data frame containing row ID annotation values every row least one input touched user.

title: Annotator table — server — annotator_table_server
code: annotator_table_server(   id,   source_data,   row_id,   col_specs,   reactable_theme = theme_bare,   reactable_options = list(),   initial_values = reactive(NULL) )

title: Annotator table — server — annotator_table_server
text: id `character(1)`. Shiny module namespace ID. Must match `id` passed [annotator_table_ui()]. source_data `reactive`. reactive expression returning source data frame. Column names must include column named `row_id` columns referenced display col_specs. row_id `character(1)`. name column `source_data` uniquely identifies row (e.g. `"id"` `"car_name"`). col_specs `list`. list column specification lists describing every column show table. entry must `name` `type`. Supported types additional fields: `"display"` Read-column sourced `source_data`. `name`     must match column data frame. `"select"` Dropdown. Requires `choices`: named character vector. `"checkbox"` Checkbox. additional fields required. `"text"` Free-text input. Optionally accepts `placeholder`. `"number"` Numeric input. Optionally accepts `min` `max`. `"radio"` Radio button group. Requires `choices`: named     character vector. types accept optional `label` (defaults `name`) optional `width` pixels. reactable_theme [reactable::reactableTheme()] object applied rendered table. Defaults `theme_bare`. reactable_options `list`. Additional arguments passed [reactable::reactable()] via [base::.call()]. initial_values `reactive`. reactive returning data frame pre-existing annotation values seed module. Defaults `reactive(NULL)`.

title: Annotator table — server — annotator_table_server
text: [shiny::reactive()] returning data frame `row_id`   column input annotation columns, filtered rows least   one input touched. Display columns included   return value.

title: Annotator table — server — annotator_table_server
text: Annotations preserved across reactive changes `source_data` — source data filtered previously annotated row disappears, annotation retained restored row reappears.

title: Annotator table — server — annotator_table_server
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ col_specs <- list(   list(name = "car",      type = "display",  label = "Car"),   list(name = "mpg",      type = "display",  label = "MPG"),   list(name = "category", type = "select",   label = "Category",        choices = c("Cheap" = "cheap", "Expensive" = "expensive")),   list(name = "approved",  type = "checkbox", label = "Approved?"),   list(name = "notes",     type = "text",     label = "Notes") )  ui <- bslib::page_fluid(   annotator_table_ui("cars") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   source <- reactive(mtcars)    result <- annotator_table_server(     id          = "cars",     source_data = source,     row_id      = "car_name",     col_specs   = col_specs   )    observe(print(result())) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } # }

title: Annotator table — UI — annotator_table_ui

title: Flexible editable table — server — flexible_table_server
text: Server-side logic Shiny module renders editable [reactable::reactable()] table. row contains one dropdown per column, built `col_specs` provide. Rows can added, deleted, reset. Columns can optionally depend : selecting value parent column filters choices child column clears stale selection.

title: Flexible editable table — server — flexible_table_server
code: flexible_table_server(   id,   col_specs,   initial_data = NULL,   reactable_theme = theme_bare,   duplicates_allowed = TRUE )

title: Flexible editable table — server — flexible_table_server
text: id `character(1)`. Shiny module namespace ID. Must match `id` passed [flexible_table_ui()]. col_specs `list`. list column specification lists. element describes one column may contain: `name` `character(1)`. Column variable name (used data     frame column name construct Shiny input IDs). `label` `character(1)`. Header label displayed table. `choices` Named character vector, named list named vectors     option-group dropdowns. `default` *(optional)* `character(1)`. Pre-selected value new     rows. Defaults `""` (blank placeholder). `width` *(optional)* `numeric(1)`. Column width pixels. `depends_on` *(optional)* `character(1)`. `name` another     column whose selected value controls choices column. `get_filtered_choices` *(optional)* `function(dependency_value)`.     Called current value `depends_on` column; must return     choices vector/list format `choices`. initial_data `reactive` `NULL`. reactive expression returning data frame used pre-populate table. Column names must match `name` fields `col_specs`. Re-fires whenever reactive changes, replacing current table contents. empty data frame (`nrow == 0`) treated reset signal. Defaults `NULL` (table starts one blank row). reactable_theme [reactable::reactableTheme()] object applied rendered table. Defaults `theme_bare`, minimal transparent theme defined `themes.R`. duplicates_allowed `logical(1)`. `FALSE`, duplicate rows removed returned reactive data frame. Defaults `TRUE`.

title: Flexible editable table — server — flexible_table_server
text: [shiny::reactive()] returns data frame current table   contents. Internal helper columns (`id`, `delete`) excluded. Rows   cell blank, `NA`, `NULL` also excluded.

title: Flexible editable table — server — flexible_table_server
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ col_specs <- list(   list(     name    = "fruit",     label   = "Fruit",     choices = c("None" = "", "Apple" = "apple", "Banana" = "banana")   ),   list(     name    = "colour",     label   = "Colour",     choices = c("None" = "", "Red" = "red", "Yellow" = "yellow")   ) )  ui <- bslib::page_fluid(   do.call(tagList, flexible_table_ui("fruit_picker")) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   selected <- flexible_table_server("fruit_picker", col_specs)    observe({     print(selected())  # data frame of complete, non-blank rows   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } # }

title: Flexible editable table — UI — flexible_table_ui
text: Returns named list UI elements flexible table module. caller responsible placing element layout. Separating elements way lets caller position table buttons independently (e.g. buttons sidebar, table main panel).

title: Flexible editable table — UI — flexible_table_ui
code: flexible_table_ui(id)

text: named list three elements: `table` [reactable::reactableOutput()] placeholder. `add_row_button` [shiny::actionButton()] appends blank row. `reset_button` [shiny::actionButton()] clears table       single blank row.

title: Flexible editable table — UI — flexible_table_ui
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In your UI definition: ui <- bslib::page_fluid(   flexible_table_ui("fruit_picker")$add_row_button,   flexible_table_ui("fruit_picker")$reset_button,   flexible_table_ui("fruit_picker")$table ) } # }

what: Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions — make_badge_renderer
title: Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions — make_badge_renderer
text: Returns `function(value)` suitable use `cell` argument [reactable::colDef()], `cell` inside `col_def_options` list passed [annotator_table_server()].

title: Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions — make_badge_renderer
code: make_badge_renderer(   badge_colors,   fallback = list(bg = "#F1EFE8", text = "#444441") )

title: Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions — make_badge_renderer
text: badge_colors Named list. name possible cell value; element `list(bg = "#hex", text = "#hex")` colour spec. fallback `list`. Colour spec used value absent `badge_colors`. Defaults `list(bg = "#F1EFE8", text = "#444441")`.

title: Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions — make_badge_renderer
text: `function(value)` returns [shiny::tags]`$span` tag.

title: Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions — make_badge_renderer
text: returned function wraps cell value `<span>` `.rs-badge` CSS class (layout constants) inline `background` / `color` styles drawn `badge_colors`. Separating structural styles (padding, border-radius, font-size) CSS dynamic colours (kept inline) means callers can restyle badges via `custom.css` without touching R code. [milestone_tracker_server()] calls function internally Card 1. Callers want appearance `annotation_col_specs` Card 2 can call pass result: “`r renderer <- make_badge_renderer(badge_colors) list(   name = "category",   type = "display",   col_def_options = list(cell = renderer) ) “`

title: Create a badge cell renderer for reactable column definitions — make_badge_renderer
code: badge_colors <- list( Cognitive = list(bg = "#E6F1FB", text = "#0C447C"), Communication = list(bg = "#EAF3DE", text = "#27500A") ) renderer <- make_badge_renderer(badge_colors) renderer("Cognitive") # Cognitive #> Cognitive

title: Milestone Tracker — Server — milestone_tracker_server
text: Milestone Tracker — Server

title: Milestone Tracker — Server — milestone_tracker_server
code: milestone_tracker_server(   id,   data,   row_id,   group_col,   group_sort_col = NULL,   item_col,   badge_col = NULL,   badge_colors = NULL,   annotation_col_specs,   card1_title = "Select Items",   card2_title = "Annotations",   group_label = NULL,   item_label = NULL,   count_label = "items",   card1_reactable_options = list() )

title: Milestone Tracker — Server — milestone_tracker_server
text: id `character(1)`. Shiny module namespace ID. data `reactive`. reactive returning source data frame. Must contain `row_id`, `group_col`, `item_col`, optionally `badge_col`. row_id `character(1)`. Name unique ID column `data` (e.g. `"id"`). group_col `character(1)`. Column group rows Card 1 (e.g. `"age"`). Clicking group header expands rows. group_sort_col `character(1)` `NULL`. Column used sort groups (e.g. `"age_months"`). `NULL`, groups sort `group_col`. item_col `character(1)`. Column containing item text shown row (e.g. `"milestone"`). badge_col `character(1)` `NULL`. Column rendered coloured badge Card 1 (e.g. `"category"`). `NULL` disables badge rendering. badge_colors Named list `NULL`. Maps badge values `list(bg = "#hex", text = "#hex")` colour specs. Required `badge_col` `NULL`. Also used colour per-group selection summary badges Card 1 — group name matches key `badge_colors`, colour applied; otherwise neutral green fallback used. annotation_col_specs `list`. Full col_specs list passed [annotator_table_server()] Card 2. Include display col_specs columns want show selected data, followed input col_specs annotation fields (select, checkbox, text, etc.). Use [make_badge_renderer()] obtain cell renderer consistent Card 1 display col_spec shows badge column. card1_title `character(1)`. Header text Card 1. card2_title `character(1)`. Header text Card 2. group_label `character(1)` `NULL`. Display label group column. Defaults `group_col`. item_label `character(1)` `NULL`. Display label item column. Defaults `item_col`. count_label `character(1)`. Suffix used aggregate row count (e.g. `"milestones"` → `"5 milestones"`). card1_reactable_options `list`. Additional arguments passed [reactable::reactable()] Card 1 via [base::.call()]. include `data`, `groupBy`, `selection`, `onClick`, `columns` — owned module. Useful overriding `defaultPageSize`, `sortable`, `theme`, etc.

title: Milestone Tracker — Server — milestone_tracker_server
text: [shiny::reactive()] returning data frame row IDs   annotation values rows least one input touched.   Passes return value [annotator_table_server()].

title: Milestone Tracker — UI — milestone_tracker_ui
text: Returns two-column bslib card layout. Card 1 holds grouped selection table (per-group selection summary ); Card 2 holds annotation table (empty-state prompt).

title: Milestone Tracker — UI — milestone_tracker_ui
code: milestone_tracker_ui(id, col_widths = c(6, 6))

title: Milestone Tracker — UI — milestone_tracker_ui
text: id `character(1)`. Shiny module namespace ID. col_widths `numeric(2)`. Bootstrap column widths two cards, passed directly [bslib::layout_columns()]. Defaults `c(6, 6)` equal split. Use e.g. `c(4, 8)` Card 2 many columns.

title: Milestone Tracker — UI — milestone_tracker_ui
text: [bslib::layout_columns()] UI element.
